title: salix × mollissima var. undulata
intro: Salix × mollissima var. undulata, also known as the wavy-leaved willow or silver willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is native to China and is a hybrid of the white willow (Salix alba) and the black willow (Salix nigra). The plant's bark contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it a valuable medicinal plant. The medicinal properties of Salix × mollissima var. undulata have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being by reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the digestive system.
